title: centaurea alba subsp. albanica
intro: Centaurea alba subsp. albanica, also known as Albanian knapweed, is a rare and endemic plant species native to Albania, characterized by its white flowers and tall stem. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Centaurea alba subsp. albanica can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
